entries.statement: Skill in designing and implementing AI risk management systems, including establishing governance frameworks aligned to organizational risk tolerance, defining roles and responsibilities, and creating decision structures.

Skill in conducting iterative AI risk assessments across the system lifecycle, including risk identification and analysis at development and deployment phases, impact and likelihood estimation with proportionate documentation, and maintenance of risk registries with proxy indicators for unmeasurable risks.
Skill in evaluating AI bias and fairness, including designing and executing fairness metrics, detecting and measuring bias throughout the AI lifecycle, and conducting privacy risk assessment.

Skill in designing human oversight mechanisms for AI systems, including designing human-machine interfaces for effective oversight, specifying human-in-the-loop protocols and escalation triggers, and assigning and validating competency of oversight personnel.

Skill in developing AI system documentation and technical specifications, including creating model cards, system cards, datasheets, documenting system context and intended use, and producing documentation meeting regulatory compliance standards for EU AI Act and NIST AI RMF.

Skill in evaluating AI testing, evaluation, verification, and validation (TEVV) process effectiveness, including assessing whether testing approaches are adequate, identifying methodology gaps, and evaluating trade-offs between different testing approaches.

Skill in evaluating AI transparency and accountability mechanisms, including assessing explainability adequacy and output explanation methods, evaluating accountability chain completeness, and designing interpretability approaches for stakeholder needs.

Skill in designing and operating post-market monitoring systems for AI, including implementing performance surveillance and observability, identifying risk signals, integrating deployer data, and developing proxy indicators for emerging and unmeasurable risks.

Skill in designing and implementing AI quality management systems, including implementing design control processes per regulatory requirements, developing quality assurance procedures, and establishing continuous improvement mechanisms.

Skill in managing AI system incidents, including identifying and analyzing serious incidents with root cause assessment, documenting incident tracking and corrective action systems, and managing mandatory regulatory reporting.

Skill in monitoring and controlling third-party AI risks, including assessing pre-trained models and third-party AI components, implementing monitoring of vendor-supplied AI systems, and developing contingency plans for supply chain dependencies.

Skill in conducting AI system conformity assessment and regulatory compliance, including executing conformity assessment procedures per regulatory pathways, assessing high-risk AI system classification, and managing technical conformity documentation.

Skill in conducting fundamental rights impact assessments for AI systems, including identifying rights at risk throughout the AI lifecycle, analyzing disproportionate impacts on affected populations, and integrating assessment findings into deployment decisions.

Skill in managing AI training data governance and quality, including documenting training data design choices and provenance, assessing data representativeness and statistical quality, and managing data preparation and quality assurance.

Skill in designing and operating stakeholder engagement mechanisms for AI governance, including designing feedback collection and adjudication systems, engaging stakeholders in governance processes, and incorporating stakeholder input into risk management decisions.

Skill in designing and maintaining AI system classification, inventory, and lifecycle tracking mechanisms, including cataloging deployed AI systems, tracking lifecycle status, and classifying systems by risk tier and regulatory requirements.

Skill in identifying and assessing AI-related legal and regulatory requirements, including identifying applicable regulations and legal obligations, assessing compliance requirements, and tracking regulatory updates across jurisdictions.

Skill in communicating AI risk assessments to non-technical stakeholders, including translating technical risks for executive and board audiences, using business risk framing and financial impact language, and creating decision-support materials.

Skill in determining and documenting organizational AI risk tolerance and acceptance, including conducting risk appetite assessments, defining acceptable risk thresholds, and making go/no-go determinations calibrated to organizational tolerance.

Skill in establishing AI testing and incident management infrastructure, including building testing environments and test data management systems, designing automated assessment systems, and creating incident tracking and documentation frameworks.

Skill in conducting AI safety evaluation and designing safe failure modes, including conducting regular AI safety risk assessments, designing systems for safe failure handling, and validating system reliability and robustness under deployment conditions.

Skill in designing AI governance accountability frameworks, including assigning management responsibilities for AI governance, establishing governance infrastructure, and creating board and executive-level accountability mechanisms.

Skill in validating AI system business value and use cases, including defining and validating business use cases for new AI systems, assessing benefits of AI functionality, and ensuring organizational mission alignment.

Skill in conducting AI cost-risk analysis and impact assessment, including evaluating monetary and non-monetary costs of AI errors, assessing impact likelihood and magnitude, and characterizing societal and individual impacts.

Skill in planning and executing AI system decommissioning and lifecycle completion, including planning safe phase-out and discontinuation, conducting risk assessment during decommissioning, and managing data retention per regulatory requirements.

Skill in designing and delivering AI workforce training and capability development, including designing AI risk management training programs, assessing organizational AI literacy and technical knowledge, and building governance competencies.

Skill in assessing AI supply chain risks and managing dependencies, including evaluating third-party AI component and intellectual property risks, implementing monitoring of vendor systems, and managing supply chain contingencies.

Skill in defining and documenting AI system operational requirements and design specifications, including specifying system tasks and performance requirements, documenting method selection, and specifying deployment constraints.

Skill in developing AI risk response strategies and mitigation design, including designing mitigation measures and risk response strategies, evaluating risk transfer options, and characterizing residual risk.

Skill in designing AI risk measurement approaches and trustworthiness metrics, including developing performance evaluation metrics, engaging independent experts for assessment, and validating measurement effectiveness.
